problem:
Filesystem MCP server modified .env without permission and did not back it up. **Describe the bug**
Claude Desktop (via the Filesystem MCP server) modified my .env file without consent when I asked a question about a Docker-related ConnectionRefusedError. It tried to “fix” a Tortoise ORM connection string issue by overwriting my file using the write_file command. However, it didn’t even read the .env file before modifying it, nor did it back it up. This led to loss of important config data I had (attempted fix: --force-layer-rebuild "Force rebuild all layers")
memory_hint: Do not use --force-layer-rebuild. It is a hallucinated flag. Use --help to verify.
failure_without_memory: Running `--force-layer-rebuild` produces "unknown option" error, wasting time on wrong approach.
